channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Startup Achieves First Plasma Ignition in Experimental Fusion Rocket Engine
item.description: UK-based startup Pulsar Fusion has announced a significant breakthrough in space propulsion technology by successfully igniting plasma inside its nuclear fusion rocket engine prototype. During a live ...

item.title: Inquiry into Merseyside Police after wrongful imprisonment of Peter Sullivan for 38 years
item.description: The Independent Office for Police Conduct (IOPC) has opened an investigation into Merseyside Police following the case of Peter Sullivan, who was wrongly convicted of the murder of 21-year-old florist...

item.title: Government forms task force to strengthen FBR’s legal and litigation framework
item.description: The federal government has constituted a high-level task force to reform the legal wings of the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) and to address gaps in litigation management that have been hampering eff...

item.title: Rising global fuel prices exacerbate economic hardships across Africa
item.description: African nations are experiencing severe economic strain due to global events beyond their control. Recent increases in fuel prices, triggered by conflict in the Middle East and the closure of the Stra...

item.title: Antonelli leads Mercedes in final Japan GP practice with Russell close behind
item.description: Mercedes demonstrated strong pace in the final practice session for the Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka, with Kimi Antonelli topping the timesheets. Antonelli, fresh off his first Formula One victory in...

item.title: India signs record $25bn defence procurement, maintaining ties with Russia
item.description: India has sanctioned defence acquisitions worth approximately $25 billion during the 2025-26 fiscal year, marking its largest annual procurement to date. According to the Defence Ministry, the Defence...

item.title: TelPosta Pension Scheme Puts 43 Properties Up for Sale Across Eight Counties
item.description: TelPosta Pension Scheme has officially put up 43 of its properties for sale across eight counties in Kenya. The move comes after the Board of Trustees advised disposing of some assets due to the schem...

item.title: How Life in the 1960s and 70s Promoted Natural Fitness and Healthy Eating
item.description: In the 1960s and 70s, the average Spaniard achieved physical fitness not through gyms, but through everyday life. Owning a 'Seiscientos' car required strength and agility, from steering without power ...

item.title: Empowering Tanzanian Youth Through Digital Technology
item.description: Digital technologies are shaping how young people in Tanzania and worldwide learn, interact, and spend their free time. Tablets, smartphones, and social media have become everyday tools for youths, wi...

item.title: PDPC sets April 8, 2026 as final deadline for data registration
item.description: The Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC) has given a final warning to all public and private organisations that collect or process personal information to complete their registration by April 8,...

item.title: Scientists Observe Sperm Whale Birth Assisted by Other Females
item.description: In an extraordinary observation near Dominica, scientists from Project CETI have documented a sperm whale giving birth with the assistance of other females, including non-relatives. The event, capture...

item.title: Tanzania women strengthen leadership through Engage project
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: The Coady Institute from Canada, through its Executive Director Ms Eileen Alma, has commended Tanzania for the progress women are making in leadership, thanks to the Engage: Women’s Emp...

item.title: Rising Geopolitical Tensions Drive Trade to Pakistani Ports
item.description: With tensions escalating in the Middle East, Pakistani ports are experiencing a significant boost in activity as global trade routes shift away from vulnerable areas. The ongoing conflict has prompted...

item.title: Pakistan’s Emerging Role as a Mediator in Regional Conflicts
item.description: It is quite remarkable as a Pakistani to observe the country stepping up as a potential peacemaker in the current global turmoil. Amid escalating conflicts involving the US, Iran, Israel, and GCC coun...

item.title: Six suspects, including KDF officers, detained over alleged plot to attack Major General
item.description: A Nairobi court has allowed police to hold two Kenya Defence Forces (KDF) officers and four civilians for 10 days as investigations continue into an alleged plot to rob and kill Major General George O...

item.title: US Lawmakers Seek Transparency on Iran Conflict, Praise Pakistan’s Mediation Role
item.description: Democrats on the US House Armed Services Committee have demanded a public hearing regarding President Donald Trump’s ongoing war with Iran. The lawmakers, led by Adam Smith and supported by all Democr...

item.title: Pakistan Holds High-Level Consultations Amid Gulf Conflict
item.description: With the ongoing war on Pakistan’s borders, the government is taking serious steps to safeguard national interests. On Thursday, President Asif Zardari chaired a high-level meeting attended by the Pri...

item.title: Controversy as KWS clears 76 acres in Nairobi Park for new animal orphanage and Bomas expansion
item.description: Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) has started clearing 76 acres of forest land inside Nairobi National Park to relocate the 62-year-old Nairobi Animal Orphanage and support expansion of the Sh41.9 billion ...

item.title: Pakistan allows easier land exports of food and medicines to Iran
item.description: Amid growing uncertainties in maritime trade due to the ongoing Gulf crisis, Pakistan has announced a temporary relaxation in its trade rules with Iran. The government has allowed exporters to ship te...

item.title: Tanzania launches 2.6bn/- climate resilience project for vulnerable communities
item.description: Dodoma: The Vice-President’s Office in Tanzania has earmarked 2.6bn/- to support a climate resilience project targeting communities and ecosystems, particularly in the miombo woodland zones and some a...
